conjecture_title: Open Problem: Finding a Shortest Even Hole
summary: The problem of finding a shortest even hole in polynomial time was resolved by Cheong and Lu in a paper posted to arXiv on August 15, 2020 — roughly one month before Cook and Seymour posed it as an open problem — giving an O(n^31) algorithm; Chiu, Lai, and Lu subsequently improved this to O(n^23) in July 2022.

since_posted.title: Finding a Shortest Even Hole in Polynomial Time
since_posted.claim: Gives the first polynomial-time algorithm (O(n^31)) for finding a shortest even hole in an n-vertex graph; this preprint was posted on August 15, 2020, about one month before Cook–Seymour's paper posed the problem.

since_posted.title: Improved Algorithms for Recognizing Perfect Graphs and Finding Shortest Odd and Even Holes
since_posted.authors: Yung-Chung Chiu, Kai-Yuan Lai, Hsueh-I Lu
since_posted.claim: Improves the running time for finding a shortest even hole from O(n^31) (Cheong–Lu) to O(n^23), along with improvements for odd holes and perfect graph recognition.

search_queries: shortest even hole algorithm polynomial time graph Cook Seymour 2009.05691
"shortest even hole" graph algorithm polynomial time 2021 2022 2023 2024
"Improved Algorithms" "perfect graphs" "shortest odd and even holes" arXiv 2022 2023
notes: The Cheong–Lu solution (arXiv:2008.06740, submitted August 15, 2020) predates the Cook–Seymour open problem posting (arXiv:2009.05691, submitted September 12, 2020) by approximately one month; Cook and Seymour were likely unaware of this concurrent work when writing. The problem is therefore fully solved. Cheong–Lu's journal version appeared in Journal of Graph Theory in 2022.
